title: Novavax Q2 2024 Earnings: Strong Partnership Revenue and Shift in Financial Composition
text: Novavax reported strong second-quarter performance, generating $415 million in total revenue and maintaining $1.1 billion in cash and equivalents. The company achieved net income of $162 million, compared to $58 million in the prior-year period. Revenue composition shifted significantly due to the Sanofi partnership. Product sales declined to $19.9 million from $285.2 million year-over-year, while licensing and royalty revenue surged to $395.6 million, primarily reflecting the $500 million upfront payment from Sanofi. Sanofi partnership: Novavax received $570 million from Sanofi ($500 million upfront plus $70 million equity investment). Beginning January 1, 2025, Sanofi assumes primary commercial responsibility for the updated COVID-19 vaccine in the U.S. and Europe. The company is eligible for up to $700 million in development, regulatory, and launch milestones. Clinical pipeline: The company plans to initiate Phase 3 trials for its COVID-19-Influenza Combination vaccine and stand-alone influenza candidates in Q4 2024, with data anticipated by mid-2025. Operational efficiency: Combined R&D and SG&A expenses decreased 34% year-over-year. Novavax targets expenses below $500 million in 2025 and below $350 million in 2026, with Sanofi reimbursing over $50 million annually. Updated 2024 guidance: Total revenue $400-$600 million, Product sales $275-$375 million, Licensing/royalties ~$425 million, Combined R&D and SG&A $700-$750 million.
